content.content: I really liked the T-shirt I bought last month. Could you please buy two more for me?

<function_thought>
To assist Emma Johnson in purchasing two more T-shirts, the first step is to view her order history to retrieve information about the T-shirt she liked. This will help in identifying the product name or ID to then search for it. I've initialized a trajectory to view her order history and will proceed with that first.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[view_order_history()]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
The order history reveals that the T-shirt Emma liked is the "Adidas Mens Z.n.e. T-Shirt" with product ID "P030." Next, I will search for this T-shirt to ensure it's available for purchase.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[search_product(product_name="Adidas Mens Z.n.e. T-Shirt")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

{'Adidas Mens Z.n.e. T-Shirt': {'price': 17.28, 'brand': 'Adidas', 'category': 'Sports', 'product_id': 'P030', 'rating': 4.3, 'reviews': ['Good fit, nice material.', "it's a sintetic shirt not the cotton as it was written in description.", 'The shirt came with yellow stains.', "Thicker material than other fitness shirts and good quality. I like these shirts as a larger gym shirt when it's cooler out, but not wearing while working out. Color is good along with value for money."]}}
